en.title: Visualization Catalog: How LagunaBeach.md Turns Data Into Pictures
en.desc: The visual modules available for LagunaBeach.md articles — from single-stat callouts to comparison tables and timelines. Each module renders as semantic HTML that both humans and AI crawlers can read.

en.title: Laguna Art Museum
en.desc: The oldest art museum in Orange County, focused exclusively on California art from the plein air era to contemporary.

en.title: Plein Air Painting
en.desc: Laguna Beach's identity as an art colony was built on painters working outdoors in the coastal light.

en.title: Thousand Steps Beach
en.desc: A secluded beach at the base of a long staircase in South Laguna, known for its dramatic cliffs and tide pools.

en.title: Victoria Beach
en.desc: A secluded beach known for its distinctive 1926 stone tower and rocky tide pool areas.

en.title: Pageant of the Masters
en.desc: A live performance where volunteers recreate famous paintings and sculptures using real people, running annually since 1933.

en.title: Sawdust Art Festival
en.desc: An open-air art festival where Laguna Beach artists sell work directly from hand-built booths, running since 1966.

en.title: The Cliff Restaurant
en.desc: A landmark oceanfront restaurant perched on the bluffs above the Pacific, serving Laguna Beach since 1946.

en.title: Founding and Early History
en.desc: How Laguna Beach grew from an indigenous homeland to an artists colony to an incorporated city.

en.title: The 1993 Firestorm
en.desc: A wildfire that destroyed 441 homes in Laguna Beach on October 27, 1993, reshaping the city's relationship with fire and development.

en.title: Tide Pools
en.desc: Laguna Beach hosts some of Southern California's most accessible and biodiverse intertidal zones.

en.title: Whale Watching
en.desc: Gray whales migrate past Laguna Beach every winter, visible from shore and by boat.

en.title: South Laguna
en.desc: The quieter southern stretch of Laguna Beach, home to hidden beaches and residential hillside neighborhoods.

en.title: The Village
en.desc: Laguna Beach's walkable downtown core, centered on Pacific Coast Highway and Forest Avenue.

en.title: Laguna Coast Wilderness Park
en.desc: A 7,000-acre protected open space surrounding Laguna Beach with 40+ miles of trails through coastal sage scrub and oak woodland.

en.title: Top of the World
en.desc: A panoramic viewpoint and trailhead offering 360-degree views from the Pacific Ocean to the Santa Ana Mountains.

en.title: How an Article Is Born: The Editorial Pipeline Behind LagunaBeach.md
en.desc: Every article on LagunaBeach.md runs through a six-stage process: find the tension, research deeply, write the ending first, verify every claim, add visuals, and connect to the knowledge graph. This is how that pipeline works.

en.title: LagunaBeach.md: How This Project Works
en.desc: An open-source knowledge base about Laguna Beach, California. Forked from Taiwan.md, built on Astro, curated by humans and AI together. This is how the system works under the hood.
